title: pedicularis resupinata subsp. resupinata
intro: Pedicularis resupinata subsp. resupinata, also known as Lop Host or Pedicularis, is a species of flowering plant in the balm family (Lamiaceae) native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin infections. The health benefits of Pedicularis resupinata subsp. resupinata include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The herbal preparations of Pedicularis resupinata subsp. resupinata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
